semantic.role: SQLite WAL recovery utility for the infra database layer. Performs safe checkpointing of uncommitted WAL data before the main database connection is opened.
responsibilities: Detect stale Write-Ahead Log (WAL) files by comparing file size against a threshold (512KB).
Safely checkpoint the WAL into the main database file using a temporary SQLite connection.
Clean up WAL and Shared Memory (SHM) files after successful recovery.
outOfScope: Database connection lifecycle management beyond recovery.
Schema migrations, queries, or transaction management.
invariants: Must be called before opening the primary database connection.
Assumes no other process has an open connection to the database at the time of recovery.

changeIntent.architecturalIntent: Provides a safe, idempotent WAL recovery routine to prevent data loss from interrupted SQLite sessions. Designed as a standalone utility within the infra/db layer.
changeIntent.recentChangeIntent: The most recent commit (4a6aac8) is a general release gate fix; no direct changes to this file's contract are implied.

publicSurface.description: Checks the WAL file size at the given database path and, if it exceeds the 512KB threshold, opens a temporary SQLite connection to checkpoint the WAL into the main database. Afterwards, removes the WAL and SHM files. Called by database initialization code before opening the primary connection.
